title: Rolling-origin CatBoost refresh with seed-consensus blending
rationale: The parent diagnostic showed that recent expanding-window CatBoost control outperformed both the physical-feature branch and the frozen current H2 surface, suggesting iteration and calibration staleness rather than missing physical features.
expected_signal: A Q2-selected seed-consensus blend changing at most 25% of rows must improve H2 score, NMAE and FICR, remain positive across seeds and at least four months, and pass the day-bootstrap gate.
risk_notes: The parent full replacement was seed-unstable; this child uses unanimous seed direction, uncertainty, disagreement, and coverage gates.
